mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in declining rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tightening labor markets that drive wage growth and consumer spending, providing a positive earnings surprise catalyst for equities over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in forward 60-day returns, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side efficiency gains, which compress input cost inflation and protect corporate profit margins, supporting equity returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negatively correlated with INDPRO growth during periods of stable CPI, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation readings that exceed a 0.4 percent monthly threshold trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the negative return differential between high-inflation and low-inflation regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline confirm a tightening labor market that boosts household income and consumption, creating a positive momentum feedback loop that drives short-term equity outperformance.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict forward 60-day returns better than a random baseline, or the marginal effect of PAYEMS becomes negative when UNRATE is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth provides a fundamental baseline trend for corporate earnings, where positive GDP acceleration mechanically translates into higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in positive GDP growth quarters are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing market acceleration signals improved credit availability and consumer confidence, which historically precede broad economic expansion and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month window.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are flat or negative when HOUST exceeds the threshold, or the signal's predictive power disappears when controlling for FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL.
